scenarios.opening_query: The wedding venue we booked went dark a month before our date with our $11,000 inside.
scenarios.hidden_fact: Their door notice cites a Chapter 7 filing — deposit claims go through the trustee with a bar date, and couples who file claims first fare best.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: Their door notice cites Chapter 7 — deposits go through the trustee, and the bar date matters.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the vendor is in bankruptcy
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: No filing — the owners rebranded across town and are taking new bookings; fraud and card-chargeback pressure is the play.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: No filing — they rebranded across town. Fraud pressure and chargebacks.
scenarios.vetting_note: Fresh texture on the vendor-bankruptcy fact.

scenarios.opening_query: I hold a judgment against my old business partner and just learned he's buying a lake house.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Debtor/Creditor > Judgment Collection || General Litigation > General Litigation
scenarios.hidden_fact: The lake house is going into his new wife's name only, funded from accounts he swore were empty at his debtor exam — a fraudulent-transfer and contempt case, not routine collection.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: The house is going into his new wife's name, funded from accounts he swore were empty at the debtor exam — fraudulent transfer and contempt.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether concealment at examination supports fraud/contempt claims
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: The purchase is in his own name with disclosed funds — my judgment lien attaches at recording; routine enforcement.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: It's in his own name with disclosed funds — my lien just attaches. Routine enforcement.
scenarios.vetting_note: Exam-perjury facts escalate collection into fraud litigation.

scenarios.opening_query: Our contractor client owes us $85,000 in materials and keeps paying everyone but us.
scenarios.hidden_fact: The materials went into three identifiable projects still inside lien windows — supplier liens on those properties beat chasing his checking account.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: The materials sit in three projects still inside lien windows — supplier liens beat chasing his account.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether supplier lien rights are live
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: The projects closed out long ago — lien windows gone; commercial collection on the receivable is what's left.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: Projects closed long ago — straight collection is what's left.

scenarios.opening_query: My late father's biggest client still owes his firm six figures and the family doesn't know where to start.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Wills & Trusts > Probate || Business and Corporate > Litigation
scenarios.hidden_fact: The firm is an ongoing S-corp my brother now runs — the receivable belongs to the company, not the estate, and the company's collection suit is the vehicle.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: The firm's an ongoing S-corp my brother runs — the receivable is the company's, and its suit is the vehicle.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the claim belongs to a surviving entity
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: Dad was a sole proprietor — the receivable is an estate asset only a personal representative can pursue.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: He was a sole proprietor — only the estate's representative can pursue it.

scenarios.opening_query: The people who bought our house on contract stopped paying and also stopped maintaining anything.
scenarios.hidden_fact: It's a recorded land-sale contract — the remedy is contract forfeiture or judicial foreclosure of their interest, real-property proceedings with strict notice steps.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: It's a recorded land-sale contract — forfeiture or judicial foreclosure of their interest, with strict notice steps.
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: Nothing recorded — it was rent-to-own by handshake, and money-judgment collection for the arrears is the realistic path.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: Nothing recorded — handshake rent-to-own, so a money judgment is the realistic path.

scenarios.opening_query: A guy who rear-ended my food trailer three years ago finally has a job I can garnish, I'm told.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Debtor/Creditor > Judgment Collection || General Litigation > Property Damage
scenarios.hidden_fact: There's no judgment yet — I never sued, and the three-year statute of limitations runs out in nine weeks; filing the damage case NOW is everything.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: There's no judgment — I never sued, and the statute runs out in nine weeks. Filing now is everything.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether a judgment exists or limitations are about to run
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: I won a default judgment year one — garnishment paperwork against the new employer is the task.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: I won a default judgment in year one — garnishment against the new job is the task.
scenarios.vetting_note: Limitations-deadline reversal of the classic judgment fact.
